_comment: Salaried management roster. Annual figures are gross pay (excluding super). Deputy returns Cost=0 for all these employees because they're not on hourly rates in Deputy — this config lets the aggregator cost their shifts. Per Xero payroll (2026-07-15) a salaried employee is paid annual/52 EVERY week regardless of hours logged, so hours decide WHERE the cost lands (pro-rata across logged shifts), not how much it is. See scripts/wage_model.py.

297.name: Pujan Tamang
297._added: 2026-07-15 — found by Xero reconciliation. Salaried $75k, rosters in Deputy (Stow Kitchen, ~40h/wk), but was absent from this file so every shift costed $0 and his real cost fell into the corp-payroll residual.

_source_of_truth: Xero payroll, not Deputy. Deputy only knows who clocked on. Run scripts/check_salaried_roster.py to detect new salary-earners and salary changes — a salaried person missing from this file is booked at $0 and their real cost silently lands in the corp-payroll residual instead of on their venue.

_corp_payroll_only._comment: Owners. Salaried in Xero, never rostered in Deputy. Their pay belongs in corp payroll — which happens automatically: corpPayrollDaily() takes the residual of Xero group payroll MINUS Deputy group wages, so anyone absent from Deputy lands there by construction. Do NOT add them to `employees` — that would move owner salary onto venue wage lines. Listed here so the roster checker stops flagging them as missing.

_xero_exempt._comment: Deputy people Zak has CONFIRMED are correctly costed at Deputy's own rate because Xero does not pay them (2026-07-17: 'assume pedro at his deputy rates. same with long long. it's just an exception for pedro'). Listed by Deputy id so rebuild_wages stops flagging them. Anyone with hours who is NOT in Xero and NOT on this list gets shouted about — that is the point: the next person in this situation must not quietly blend into the fallback. Do NOT add someone here to make a reconciliation tie; add them only when Zak has said it's an exception.
_xero_exempt._removed_225: Long Long — REMOVED 2026-07-18. Zak: 'teramet is long long'. He IS in Xero, as Teramet Tongsong: 39 weeks, $26,293.62. He was exempted on my verification that Xero 'has never paid him under any name', which I did by SEARCHING THE NAME 'Long Long' — a test that could never have succeeded. For 39 weeks his Deputy cost sat on a venue AND his Xero pay sat in the corp-payroll residual: counted twice, while the group total tied perfectly. This entry stays as a warning: an exemption is a claim that a person and a payroll have never met, and the only cheap evidence for it is a name search, which is exactly the evidence that fails when the two systems disagree about names — i.e. always. Require week-alignment AND Zak.
_xero_exempt._2026-07-18b_xero_no_deputy: Xero PAID these two but neither has a mappable Deputy shift, so employee_map can't reach them. Classified by Zak 2026-07-18, NOT lumped as 'tax/WC overhead': (1) 'Paula Tarrago' $1,964, one payroll week Nov-2024 — she was SOCIAL MEDIA MANAGER from 2022 until ~Dec 2025; this payment was a payout for that work. Social media is a GROUP MARKETING cost, so overhead is the correct bucket (not a venue wage, not tax/WC). Since Jan 2026 the social-media role belongs to Twin Fin, a CONTRACTOR paid via invoices (AP), not payroll — so it never appears in this wage reconciliation at all; it's a marketing overhead going forward. (2) 'Rhianna Grove' $563, one week Apr-2026 — she was STOW staff; her Deputy account was deleted or never onboarded, so there is no Deputy day to pin it to. It BELONGS on Stowaway; at $563 for a single historical week it isn't worth a bespoke injection mechanism, but it is Stow's, not overhead. Recorded here so both are known, not a mystery gap. If Rhianna recurs, build the Xero->venue override then.
_xero_exempt._2026-07-18: Zak: 'the rest just cost out at deputy rates'. Costing them at Deputy's rate was ALREADY the behaviour — it is the fallback — so listing them here changes no number; it only stops the weekly NOT IN XERO warning, which IS the point: a permanent false positive is how a report gets ignored. I claimed these were 'verified safe' because no Wilson/Patrick/Sanjida appears among the unmapped Xero payees. Long Long (removed above) proves that reasoning is WEAK: he was 'not in Xero under any name' right up until Zak said he was Teramet Tongsong. A name search cannot clear anyone when the two systems disagree about names. What these three DO have now is week-alignment run against every unmapped Xero payee with no candidate returned — better evidence, still not proof of a negative. If any of them turns out to be someone, expect a double count of their Deputy cost against 213/221/252's real payslips.
